raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Legislation AB 110 was passed to implement standard fraud-prevention practices at California's Employment Development Department (EDD).",
    "EDD lost more than $810 million in taxpayer dollars last year due to fraud.",
    "AB 110 requires EDD to cross-check incarceration records with unemployment claims to prevent paying fraudulent benefits.",
    "California law restricts inmate information from being shared with other agencies, differing from practices in other states.",
    "AB 110 aims to enable EDD to cross-check claimants' information against incarceration data maintained by the California Department of Corrections and Rehabilitation (CDCR).",
    "The prison-based fraud that affected EDD was described as outrageous and egregious.",
    "Cross-checking unemployment claims against incarceration data is proposed as a common-sense fraud prevention measure.",
    "AB 110 is expected to ensure that unemployment benefits only go to eligible individuals.",
    "The California Chamber of Commerce supports AB 110 as it facilitates sharing of information between EDD and California's correctional system.",
    "AB 110 addresses loopholes exploited by inmates to defraud the state for their personal gain.",
    "The breadth of the EDD fraud involved inmates stealing money meant for those in need."
  ]
}
```
